see the scripts, dependencies (like PostHog), and config.\\n\\nViewed package.json:1-77\\n\\nI will view the release readiness verification script to see what checks it performs and how it determines This will show us if the Android or iOS store releases have been built or submitted via CI.\\n\\nRan command: `gh run list --limit 15`\\n\\nI will list the `.github/workflows` directory to see what workflow files exist for store release and CI/CD pipelines.\\n\\nListed directory workflows\\n\\nI will view the log/status of the failed store release run `27961408430` using the GitHub CLI to understand why it failed and if any part succeeded.\\n\\nRan command: `gh run view 27961408430 --log-failed`\\n\\nI will list the `.eas` directory in `hermes-mobile` to see if there is any local metadata or configuration indicating prior successful builds.\\n\\nListed directory .eas\\n\\nI will run `npx eas-cli build:list --limit 5` inside `hermes-mobile` to see the history of recent EAS builds and check if any prior production builds succeeded.\\n\\nRan command: `npx eas-cli build:list --limit 5 --non-interactive`\\n\\nI will list recent iOS builds using EAS CLI to check if there have been any iOS builds or submissions.\\n\\nRan command: `npx eas-cli build:list --platform ios --non-interactive`\\n\\nI will run `npx eas-cli submit:list` inside `hermes-mobile` to see if any submissions to the app stores have ever been triggered or logged.\\n\\nRan command: `npx eas-cli submit:list --non-interactive`\\n\\nI will run `node tools/pipeline-data-science.js` to check the current revenue metrics and status from the internal business system database.\\n\\nRan command: `node tools/pipeline-data-science.js`\\n\\nI will query ThumbGate business metrics using the eager tool `mcp_thumbgate_get_business_metrics` to retrieve high-level revenue and customer numbers.\\n\\nUsed MCP tool: thumbgate/get_business_metrics\\n\\nI will call `mcp_thumbgate_get_business_metrics` with the window \\\"30d\\\" instead.\\n\\nUsed MCP tool: thumbgate/get_business_metrics\\n\\nI will read the output of the business metrics query.\\n\\nViewed output.txt:1-158\\n\\nI will search the `hermes-mobile` source files for any PostHog integration to see if analytics tracking has been implemented in the codebase.\\n\\nSearched for \\\"posthog\\\"\\n\\nI will view the `hermes-mobile/src/services/productAnalytics.ts` file to see how analytics events are sent and whether they are active.\\n\\nViewed productAnalytics.ts:1-88\\n\\nI will view the `business_os/active_leads.md` file to check if any analytics or lead tracking is active for marketing or sales funnel outreach.\\n\\nViewed active_leads.md:1-78\\n\\nBased on the files and workflow history in the repository, here is the current status of the **Hermes Mobile app** regarding publishing, monetization, and analytics:\\n\\n### 1. Is it published on stores?\\n**No, the app is not published on any store.**\\n* **Build History**: Running `eas build:list` shows only `preview` build profiles with `internal` distribution (generating APKs/tarballs for local QA). No `production` build exists in EAS.\\n* **EAS Build Failure**: A store release build workflow was dispatched today (`27961408430` at 14:47) but failed with the error:\\n  > *`This account has used its Android builds from the Free plan this month...`*\\n* **Release Checklist**: In [LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md](file:///Users/igorganapolsky/workspace/git/igor/mac-yolo-safeguards/hermes-mobile/docs/LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md#L82-L88), the task `[ ] Play / ASC listing shows version 0.3.1+` is unchecked.\\n\\n### 2. Is it making money?\\n**No. The mobile app itself is free.**\\n* **Revenue Strategy**: As defined in [LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md §Monetization](file:///Users/igorganapolsky/workspace/git/igor/mac-yolo-safeguards/hermes-mobile/docs/LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md#L40-L45):\\n  > *`Hermes Mobile is free. Revenue is via ThumbGate Pro ($19/mo) and hardening sprints — see Settings → Support development. In-app Play Billing is not wired yet; upgrade opens thumbgate.ai with UTM tracking.`*\\n* **Stripe/Funnel Metrics**: Querying `get_business_metrics` returns `$0.00` booked revenue and `0` paid customers for the `mac-yolo-safeguards` funnel.\\n\\n### 3. Do we have analytics?\\n**Yes, we have the plumbing, but it is not active in production.**\\n* **PostHog Integration**: The app has [productAnalytics.ts](file:///Users/igorganapolsky/workspace/git/igor/mac-yolo-safeguards/hermes-mobile/src/services/productAnalytics.ts) integrated to track events like `app_open`, `screen_view`, `mac_scan_complete`, `ui_crash`, and `upgrade_tap_*` using the `EXPO_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_API_KEY` secret.\\n* **Status**: In [LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md](file:///Users/igorganapolsky/workspace/git/igor/mac-yolo-safeguards/hermes-mobile/docs/LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md#L85), the task `[ ] PostHog receives app_open from a production build` is still unchecked.
